title: Mesangial Matrix Evaluation Protocol
author: Jeff Hodgin
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.562g9ge

steps.title: Wash 2x 4 minutes in Xylene
steps.instruction: Wash 2x 4 minutes in Xylene.

steps.title: Wash in 100% E t OH 2x minutes
steps.instruction: Wash in 100% E t OH 2x minutes

steps.title: Wash in 95% E t OH 1x 2minutes
steps.instruction: Wash in 95% E t OH 1x 2minutes.

steps.title: Wash in 70% E t OH 1x 2minutes
steps.instruction: Wash in 70% E t OH 1x 2minutes

steps.title: Rinse in dH 2 O
steps.instruction: Rinse in dH 2 O.

steps.title: Incubate in 0
steps.instruction: Incubate in 0.5% Periodic Acid solution for 5min.

steps.title: Rinse 3x in dH 2 O
steps.instruction: Rinse 3x in dH 2 O.

steps.title: Incubate in Schiff’s reagent for 15minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate in Schiff’s reagent for 15minutes

steps.title: Rinse under running lukewarm tap water for 5minutes
steps.instruction: Rinse under running lukewarm tap water for 5minutes.

steps.title: Incubate in Hemotoxylin for 90 second
steps.instruction: Incubate in Hemotoxylin for 90 second.

steps.title: Rinse 6x in dH 2 O
steps.instruction: Rinse 6x in dH 2 O.

steps.title: Wash in 70% E t OH 1x 2minutes
steps.instruction: Wash in 70% E t OH 1x 2minutes.

steps.title: Wash in 95% E t OH 1x 2minutes
steps.instruction: Wash in 95% E t OH 1x 2minutes.

steps.title: Wash in 100% E t OH 2x 2minutes
steps.instruction: Wash in 100% E t OH 2x 2minutes.

steps.title: Incubate in Xylene for at least 5minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate in Xylene for at least 5minutes.

steps.title: Mount slides with mounting medium 1 drop
steps.instruction: Mount slides with mounting medium 1 drop

steps.title: Insert cover glass carefully, avoid bubble Note
steps.instruction: Insert cover glass carefully, avoid bubble Note: Schiff is light sensitive, mutagenic and has bad smell. Use fume hood or flow hood for handling Schiff’s reagent.

steps.title: Using the camera or MetaMorph® software...
steps.instruction: Using the camera or MetaMorph® software digitizes 30 cortical glomeruli per case with a 40 X lens. Glomeruli should be chosen for a similar diameter of maximal size. Save images as uncompressed Tiff files.

steps.title: Open the glomerulus Tiff file in MetaMorph®
steps.instruction: Open the glomerulus Tiff file in MetaMorph®. Scale the image in such a way that the entire glomerulus can be seen on the screen (50-75%).

steps.title: Using the polygon tool carefully outline the glomerular tuft
steps.instruction: Using the polygon tool carefully outline the glomerular tuft. Double click to close the polygon tool.

steps.title: From the tool bar choose Measure,...
steps.instruction: From the tool bar choose Measure, Calibrate Distances and in the Apply window choose the calibration file for the camera from which the image was taken. Then choose Apply.

steps.title: The area of the glomerular tuft...
steps.instruction: The area of the glomerular tuft can then be calculated by choosing Measure from the tool bar and then Region Measurements . Record the glomerulus area displayed.

steps.title: To calculate the area that is...
steps.instruction: To calculate the area that is PAS stained the tuft will have to be removed from the background. Select the outlined tuft and then from the tool bar choose Edit, Duplicate, Image. Close the full size image.

steps.title: Size the edited image to 150 – 200 %
steps.instruction: Size the edited image to 150 – 200 %.

steps.title: From the tool bar select Measure and Set Color Threshold
steps.instruction: From the tool bar select Measure and Set Color Threshold .

steps.title: In the Set Color Threshold window select Set By Example
steps.instruction: In the Set Color Threshold window select Set By Example .

steps.title: Using Display on the tool bar...
steps.instruction: Using Display on the tool bar and Adjust Digital Contrast the color brightness and contrast can be adjusted to best suit thresholding the area of PAS stain.

steps.title: Once the image is adjusted use...
steps.instruction: Once the image is adjusted use the cursor to choose the area of staining. Continue clicking the cursor over the area until the entire PAS stained area is highlighted. As each pixel is selected every pixel that color is also selected. Care must be taken to ensure that only PAS stained tissue is highlighted. ♦ NOTE: If an area is mistakenly selected selecting Undo Last Click in the Set Color Threshold box will remove the last selection. ♦ To clear all selections check the box next to Reset color threshold range on next click in the Set Color Threshold box. The next pixel chosen in the image will clear the screen. Continue selecting pixels. ♦ To toggle between the selected and unselected screen, select OFF and Inclusive in the Set Color Threshold box

steps.title: Once all the PAS stained area...
steps.instruction: Once all the PAS stained area is selected choose Measure and Integrated Morphometry Analysis .

steps.title: In the Integrated Morphometry Analysis box...
steps.instruction: In the Integrated Morphometry Analysis box under Set Up Parameters For: choose Measuring and then Total Area and then Classifying and Total Area.

steps.title: Now choose Measure and the “area...
steps.instruction: Now choose Measure and the “area of PAS staining” will be the last cell in the Summary window under Total .

steps.title: To close the Integrated Morphometry Analysis...
steps.instruction: To close the Integrated Morphometry Analysis box choose Reset Current and then Close .

steps.title: The percent of the glomerulus that...
steps.instruction: The percent of the glomerulus that is PAS stained is calculated as:
